title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston men were arrested in connection with a June 27 shooting on Blue Hill Avenue that killed one and injured four.
Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris both face charges related to assault and battery with a dangerous weapon, authorities said.
Wilcox-Buford and Burris appeared in court, pled not guilty, and failed to post bail, with hearings set for August 4.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reports 18 cases of cyclosporiasis from May 1 to July 1, aligning with seasonal norms, says DPH.
Over 400 cyclospora infections linked to a Midwestern outbreak reported across Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and West Virginia.
Cyclospora infections in Massachusetts are typically linked to contaminated produce; DPH advises washing fresh produce thoroughly.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 brought crowds and challenges to local sailing centers, impacting summer youth programs like Courageous Sailing Center.
Courageous Sailing Center adapted by using cruising boats to let students view tall ships and taking sailing classes upriver.
Piers Park Sailing Center in East Boston taught 300 youths and shared ties with tall ships, including captains who trained there.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts issued an air quality alert statewide due to wildfire smoke from Canada, effective until 12 a.m. Thursday.
MassDEP warns the air quality is unhealthy for sensitive groups, including people with heart or lung conditions and active individuals.
Residents advised to limit outdoor activities and monitor air quality via MassDEP’s MassAir map and local health advisories.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Oosterschelde, a historic Dutch tall ship, is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026, offering public tours.
Sail Boston 2026 features over 50 tall ships from 20+ countries, celebrating America's 250th birthday with events and fireworks.
After Sail Boston, Oosterschelde will offer tours in Quincy before heading to Canada, Spain, and France in 2026.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer, who once worked at Warby Parker, now opens for Bob Dylan at Leader Bank Pavilion on July 16.
Spencer recounts meeting Dylan unexpectedly in her dressing room after a performance at Outlaw Music Fest in 2024.
The meeting led to Spencer joining Dylan's tour, marking her continued rise in the music industry.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants made OpenTable's 2026 top 100 list, including two in Boston.
Restaurants in Kennebunkport and Uncasville also ranked, highlighting dining's role in hotel booking decisions.
Hotel dining by travelers increased 13% yearly, with 60% booking hotels mainly for their restaurants, OpenTable reports.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, New England Revolution head coach, reminisces about watching the World Cup growing up in Belgrade.
Mitrović's World Cup memories include Yugoslavia's 1990 loss to Argentina, overshadowing his aunt's wedding day.
He coached Serbia to a 2015 FIFA U20 World Cup win and led the US in the 2024 Summer Olympics.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics head coach Joe Mazzulla trains in MMA with Eric Nicksick to maintain mental and physical sharpness.
Mazzulla believes the fighter's mentality helps improve his coaching by offering new perspectives on communication.
Mazzulla integrates lessons from MMA to enhance game strategy, aiming to keep the Celtics competitive.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Driver Hernan Ramon Marrero had a blood alcohol level of 0.192, more than twice the legal limit, during fatal crash.
Marrero consumed nine alcoholic drinks at a Saugus bar before driving wrong-way and colliding with Trooper Kevin Trainor's cruiser.
Trooper Trainor and Marrero both died in the Route 1 head-on collision, which occurred after Marrero ignored traffic signs.
